address: 2 Samlarn Road, Phra Singh, Muang Chiang Mai

description.en:
## Overview

Wat Phra Singh Woramahawihan is the most revered temple in Chiang Mai and a masterpiece of Lanna architecture. Sitting at the western end of Ratchadamnoen Road inside the Old City, it is both an active place of worship and one of the top cultural attractions in northern Thailand. The temple complex is spacious and beautifully maintained, with shaded grounds that invite quiet contemplation alongside the bustle of visiting tourists and praying devotees.

## History

The temple was founded in 1345 by King Phayu of the Lanna Kingdom to enshrine the ashes of his father, King Kham Fu. It was originally called Wat Li Chiang Phra but was renamed Wat Phra Singh in 1367 when the Phra Singh Buddha image, believed to have originated in Sri Lanka, was brought to the temple. Over the centuries the complex grew with the addition of the exquisite Lai Kham chapel, the towering main viharn, a library, and a large golden chedi. The temple holds the highest royal status of any temple in Chiang Mai.

## What to See

The star attraction is the Phra Singh Buddha housed inside the Lai Kham chapel, a small but magnificent building decorated with the finest examples of Lanna-era mural paintings in existence. These murals depict scenes from the Sang Thong folk tale and daily life in old Chiang Mai, with rich detail and vibrant colors that have survived centuries. The main viharn contains a large seated Buddha image and ornate wooden carvings. The golden chedi behind the main hall enshrines the ashes of the temple's royal founder. During Songkran in April, the Phra Singh image is paraded through the streets of Chiang Mai in a grand water-festival procession.

## Practical Info

The temple is open daily from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM. The entrance fee is 20 THB for foreign visitors, while the main grounds are free to walk through. A modest dress code is enforced, with shoulders and knees covered. The temple is a short walk from the Sunday Walking Street market starting point and pairs well with visits to Wat Chedi Luang and Wat Chiang Man for a comprehensive Old City temple tour. Early morning offers the most peaceful atmosphere, while late afternoon provides beautiful golden light on the gilded structures.
